summary.intent: Fix PR review comments on #613 by adding PartialEq/Eq derives to ModelCapabilities and switching test construction from default() to infer() for real model IDs, plus adding JSON output assertions.
summary.outcome: All review comments addressed: derives added, test sites updated to use infer() for real model IDs while keeping default() for fake/test model IDs, 424 tests pass, changes committed and pushed.

repo: ModelCapabilities::infer() uses model ID prefix matching (e.g. 'gemini-3', 'gpt-5.x') to populate capabilities; supports_reasoning_for_model() is the underlying function.
Tests using fake model IDs (test::auto-compact, unit-test-model, shared-model, custom-stale, remote-model) correctly use default() since infer() won't recognize them.
The mk closure pattern in provider tests takes id: &str and constructs ModelInfo — these should use infer(id) when exercising real model ID paths.
The chat list()/list_all() tests manually set capabilities fields on some models (e.g. claude-opus-4-5 had reasoning: true set explicitly) instead of using infer().

code.finding: Four mk closure sites that construct ModelInfo with real model IDs — updated from ModelCapabilities::default() to ModelCapabilities::infer(id)
list_and_list_all_return_all_registered_models test used manual field overrides for capabilities; updated to use infer() for gpt-5.2 and gemini-3-flash with JSON output assertions added

workflow: Fetch PR review comments via Bash before starting fixes to get the full list upfront.
When updating test construction sites, triage by model ID realism: real/known IDs get infer(), synthetic/fake IDs keep default().
Used a subagent for bulk chat test capability fixes to protect main context window.

friction: Required multiple Read passes on the same large files (providers/src/lib.rs read 6+ times) to locate the mk closure sites, suggesting the file is large and the relevant sections are spread out.
The claude-opus-4-5 test had manually set reasoning: true instead of using infer(), meaning the original PR left test inconsistencies that the reviewer caught.
open_items: JSON output assertions were added to some tests but the transcript doesn't confirm which specific fields/values were asserted — worth verifying coverage is complete.
GPT-5.x reasoning support was added as a fix for #602 — that issue should be closed/referenced once PR #613 merges.
